scenario.problem: Releases took 3 weeks due to manual QA gates. Developers were frustrated, and the business was losing ground to faster competitors. CTO demanded faster time-to-market without compromising security.

implementation.objective: Transform the CI/CD pipeline from a bottleneck into a competitive advantage.
implementation.kr_1: Increase Deployment Frequency from 1 per month to 2 per week.
implementation.kr_2: Reduce Lead Time for Changes from 21 days to < 48 hours.

results.automation: Implemented aggressive automated testing and isolated canary deployments.
results.business_impact: By Q2, the team deployed daily. The business launched three new loan products months ahead of schedule.

lessons: You cannot improve what you don't measure. Establishing the DORA baseline was the critical first step before any engineering work.
Pairing speed (Deployment Frequency) with a quality constraint (Failure Rate < 2%) prevents reckless behavior.
anti_patterns: Setting an OKR to 'Implement Kubernetes'. Tools are not objectives. The objective is what the tool enables (e.g., scalability, deployment speed).
